title: populus simonii var. manshurica
intro: Populus simonii var. manshurica, also known as Chinese poplar or Manshuri poplar, is a deciduous tree species within the Populus genus, native to East Asia and characterized by its broad leaves and distinctive bark. The bark of the Populus simonii var. manshurica tree has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. This plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function, making it a valuable component in traditional herbal remedies. The herbal preparation of Populus simonii var. manshurica typically involves drying and processing the bark, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, depending on the desired application and cultural tradition.
